text: Company Policy Handbook Effective Date: January 1, 2024 1. Remote Work Policy Employees may work remotely up to 3 days per week with manager approval. Remote work requests must be submitted at least 48 hours in advance through the HR portal. Employees must maintain a dedicated workspace and ensure reliable internet connectivity. Core hours of 10 AM to 3 PM must be observed for meetings and collaboration. All remote work arrangements are subject to quarterly review and may be revoked based on performance or business needs. 2. Vacation and Time Off Policy All full-time employees receive 15 days of paid vacation per year, accrued monthly. Vacation requests must be submitted at least 2 weeks in advance for periods longer than 3 days. Unused vacation days may be carried over up to 5 days to the following year. Employees with 5+ years of service receive 20 days of vacation annually. Sick leave is provided separately at 10 days per year and does not require advance notice. 3.

Sick leave is provided separately at 10 days per year and does not require advance notice. 3. Expense Reimbursement Policy Employees may submit expense reports for business-related costs through the expense management system. Receipts are required for all expenses over $25. Expense reports must be submitted within 30 days of the expense date. Manager approval is required for expenses over $500. Travel expenses must be pre-approved and follow the company travel guidelines. Meal allowances are capped at $75 per day for domestic travel and $100 per day for international travel.

4. Confidentiality and Data Security All employees must maintain strict confidentiality of company information. Sensitive data must be stored in approved systems with proper access controls. Sharing confidential information with unauthorized parties is grounds for immediate termination. Employees must report any suspected data breaches to IT Security within 24 hours. Personal devices used for work must have approved security software installed. All company data must be deleted from personal devices upon termination of employment. 5. Professional Code of Conduct Employees are expected to maintain professional behavior at all times. Discrimination, harassment, and bullying are strictly prohibited and will result in disciplinary action up to termination. All employees must complete annual ethics and compliance training. Conflicts of interest must be disclosed to HR and management. Accepting gifts valued over $100 from vendors requires prior approval.

Conflicts of interest must be disclosed to HR and management. Accepting gifts valued over $100 from vendors requires prior approval. Company resources must be used for business purposes only. 6. Performance Review Process Performance reviews are conducted bi-annually in June and December. Employees must complete self-assessments two weeks before review meetings. Goals are set collaboratively between employees and managers. Performance ratings determine annual bonus eligibility and salary adjustments. Employees rated below expectations will receive a performance improvement plan. Outstanding performers may be nominated for promotion or special recognition awards.
